strengths: 17+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput microservices — depth that maps to a Principal IC scope
Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks, demonstrating hands-on builder fluency in current AI tooling
Founder of GISCollective (open-source, Kubernetes-deployable geo-data platform used by Green Map) showing entrepreneurial ownership and end-to-end product creation
concerns: No explicit Master's/PhD in an AI-related field listed among preferred qualifications
Cloud breadth concentrated on AWS CDK; no documented multi-cloud strategic advisory footprint
Director-level IC influence (advising strategic customers, inspiring external audiences) is implied through talks rather than named in title history

key_factors.factor: Hands-on AI/agentic builder experience (MCP, agent frameworks)
15+ years polyglot engineering spanning embedded to high-scale microservices
Entrepreneurial founder track record with open-source platform shipped to real users

response.justification: The candidate brings the 15-year engineering arc the role requires, spanning embedded C/C++ at National Instruments, ultra-low-latency D microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of RPS, fintech platforms at RIDE, and cloud-native TypeScript/AWS at Optilyz — exactly the cross-stack fluency a Principal IC needs to spot differentiation opportunities. Critically, current work at re:cinq is directly on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, placing them on the frontier of agentic AI tooling that the role centers on. Founding and sustaining GISCollective for nearly a decade evidences entrepreneurial drive inside and outside larger orgs, while a decade of conference talks, workshops, and courses (FOSS4G, Selenium curriculum, Web Internals, Refactoring Dojos) demonstrates the inspire-and-share muscle the JD explicitly calls out.
